title: epipactis flaminia
intro: Epipactis flaminia, also known as the Apennine helleborine, is a species of orchid native to the Apennine Mountains in Italy. It is a terrestrial orchid with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds that provide anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, Epipactis flaminia is prepared as an infusion or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects and is used in folk medicine to treat coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
